finding_results.notes: Four Ren speeches revised to stay in her concrete/logistical register. (1) Line 307: 'I needed you to touch the part that belonged to you' (poetic thesis) replaced with tactical method explanation — 'I needed you to find your own piece first. Your locker, the precinct file — so when the rest showed up, you'd know your part wasn't forged.' (2) Line 411: Removed thesis-cap sentence 'It wasn't one thing.' — the enumeration of specific acts now ends on the concrete accusation and flows into line 415's personal admission. (3) Line 425: 'It wasn't design. It was that mess, over and over, and you still all wound up under one roof' replaced with 'Nobody designed that.' plus a thumbnail-rubbing pause and 'You just kept showing up in the same records. Same port, same—roof.' — visible self-correction at the em-dash where she catches herself reaching for domestic language; Jet's 'My what' reaction preserved. (4) Line 531: Thematic frame ('I got tired of them living only in forms' / 'That's what I wanted in the room') replaced with halting delivery — 'I just—' going straight into sensory details, then a self-correction: 'That's—I wanted them to have that. Not just the intake numbers.' Two beats now include visible self-corrections (lines 425 and 531). None of the four revised passages can be lifted as standalone theses about the crew or the system.

summary: Single finding addressed: Ren's four polished thematic mini-speeches revised into her style-bible register of calm mechanical precision with concrete/logistical vocabulary. Poetic thesis statements replaced with tactical explanations, personal admissions of failed search, fragmented trailing speech, and visible self-corrections. Surrounding reactions (Jet's 'My what,' Ren's blush, Spike's manifest focus) all preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Heading contract intact.
